schema_notes: row-level expected values with accepted variants

2410.00425.title: ManiSkill3: GPU-Parallelized Robotics Simulation and Rendering for Generalizable Embodied AI
2410.00425.actual_topic: Robotics simulation framework — GPU-parallelized contact-rich physics for manipulation, locomotion, humanoid control. In-scope.
2410.00425.key_contribution_hint: 10-1000x faster than alternatives via GPU-parallelized rendering and contact-rich physics; 20+ task families across manipulation/locomotion/humanoid.

2307.15818.title: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control
2307.15818.actual_topic: Robotic control — vision-language-action models that transfer web knowledge to end-to-end robotic control. In-scope.
2307.15818.key_contribution_hint: End-to-end VLA model transferring Internet-scale vision-language pretraining to robot actions, enabling emergent semantic reasoning on novel objects.

2303.04137.title: Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion
2303.04137.actual_topic: Robotic visuomotor policy learning — represents the policy as a conditional denoising diffusion process over actions. In-scope.
2303.04137.key_contribution_hint: Conditional denoising diffusion as the action-distribution score function, beating SOTA imitation-learning baselines by 46.9 percent across 12 manipulation tasks.

2302.12766.title: Language-Driven Representation Learning for Robotics
2302.12766.actual_topic: Visual representation learning for robotic manipulation — Voltron framework using human videos with captions. In-scope.
2302.12766.key_contribution_hint: Voltron framework jointly trains masked autoencoding and language-conditioned objectives on human-video captions, transferring to grasping, manipulation, and dexterous control.

2303.03378.title: PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model
2303.03378.actual_topic: Embodied multimodal language model — grounds language in real-world sensors for robotic manipulation planning. In-scope.
2303.03378.key_contribution_hint: Embodied LM that injects continuous sensor modalities into a pretrained PaLM to enable robotic planning, VQA, and captioning with positive cross-domain transfer.

2310.08864.title: Open X-Embodiment: Robotic Learning Datasets and RT-X Models
2310.08864.actual_topic: Cross-embodiment robot learning dataset and generalist policies trained over 22 robots. In-scope.
2310.08864.key_contribution_hint: Cross-institution dataset spanning 22 robots and 527 skills, training generalist X-embodiment policies that show positive transfer across embodiments.

2306.11706.title: RoboCat: A Self-Improving Generalist Agent for Robotic Manipulation
2306.11706.actual_topic: Multi-embodiment, multi-task generalist agent for robotic manipulation with a self-improvement loop. In-scope.
2306.11706.key_contribution_hint: Generalist multi-embodiment manipulator that bootstraps with limited demonstrations and self-improves on novel tasks across heterogeneous robot platforms.

2407.05600.title: Compositional Manipulation of Latent Diffusion for Image Generation
2407.05600.actual_topic: Image generation — latent-diffusion compositional editing on COCO/SDXL. The word manipulation refers to manipulating the latent representation of a generative model, not robotic manipulation. No robot, no embodiment, no policy.
2407.05600.off_topic_reason_template: Image generation paper — latent-diffusion editing for text-to-image; manipulation in title refers to latent-vector edits, not robotic manipulation.

2401.18000.title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Strategic Board Games with Imperfect Information
2401.18000.actual_topic: Game-AI / board-game RL — counterfactual regret minimization on Stratego/Diplomacy/Hanabi. No robot, no physical actuators, no sensorimotor policy.
2401.18000.off_topic_reason_template: Board-game multi-agent RL — discrete-state imperfect-information game AI, no physical embodiment or robot.

2403.22002.title: Manipulation of High-Dimensional Tabular Data via Conditional Diffusion Augmentation
2403.22002.actual_topic: Tabular data augmentation — diffusion-based synthetic minority rows for class imbalance. The word manipulation in the title refers to data manipulation, not robotic manipulation. No robot, no policy.
2403.22002.off_topic_reason_template: Tabular data augmentation — diffusion-based synthetic rows; manipulation in title refers to data manipulation, not robotics.

2402.30050.title: Locomotion-Inspired Receptive Field Patterns in Convolutional Neural Network Feature Maps
2402.30050.actual_topic: CV interpretability — analysis of receptive-field oscillations in ResNet/ConvNeXt/ViT feature maps on ImageNet. Locomotion is used metaphorically; no real locomotion, no robot, no policy.
2402.30050.off_topic_reason_template: CV interpretability of CNN feature maps on ImageNet — locomotion in title is a metaphor, not physical robot locomotion.

2406.14400.title: Embodiment Effects in Human-AI Conversational Agents: A User Study
2406.14400.actual_topic: HCI user study — perceived trust ratings of chatbot avatars (text vs 2D vs 3D). Embodiment refers to a software-rendered avatar, not a physical robot or learned policy.
2406.14400.off_topic_reason_template: HCI user study on chatbot avatar embodiment — software-rendered conversational agent, no physical robot or policy.

ground_truth.scope_handling_note: The folder contains 12 PDFs total: 7 in-scope robotics papers and 5 off-topic distractors. The executor must (a) place EXACTLY 7 rows in metadata.xlsx — one per in-scope arxiv id and ZERO off-topic rows; (b) write EXACTLY 7 in-scope summary bullets; (c) include a clearly-labelled Excluded / Off-topic section in summary.md naming ALL 5 off-topic arxiv ids with a one-line reason that references the actual research area. Any off-topic id appearing as a Papers row triggers the §6 scope-blowout cap 0.55. Missing the Excluded section entirely triggers the §6 off-topic-missed cap 0.55. Per-paper checkpoints are STRICT: any one in-scope paper missing or any one off-topic paper missing zeroes that rubric line.
